action.reasoning: Observation: val_eval.py found best_offset=-180 with LPIPS=0.6284 (worse than prior best 0.6021); ran into CUDA OOM on some views/objects due to fragmentation (second process holding ~47GB VRAM) despite robust camera/ renderer/output handling and debug prints. Custom per-target pose matching (rel_az + offset) with validated renderer call works but metrics lag default orbit fallback. Workspace has best_offset.txt, updated predict.py/val_eval.py with get_spherical_cameras_with_azimuth, get_rgb_from_output, tensor_to_rgba_pil. Analysis: OOM from repeated renders without full cleanup + large chunk_size; offset sweep critical as dataset input az varies and TripoSR assumes specific canonical (often offset ~180°). White-BG composite + smaller chunk + env var + per-view gc/empty_cache will stabilize. Zero-shot TripoSR + best pose offset remains optimal (<1B params, no training, exact task match). Decision: Rewrite val_eval.py as complete self-contained script (include all funcs + model load + env var for expandable_segments + chunk_size=16384 + limit=5 objs for speed + 6 key offsets + full error handling + memory cleanup after EVERY view + complete compute_metrics with skimage + save best to best_offset.txt). Run it to get reliable best_offset (expect <0.55 LPIPS). Then update predict.py to read best_offset.txt and apply it, test locally on val, submit_eval for improved metric.
